purpose: *** For every approved remediation item: EVERY affected element, CLASSIFIED. This is the surface Cycle 7 diffs against. An element not listed here is, by construction, OUT OF SCOPE. ***

classification.READ_ONLY: the slice reads it; does not change it. *** Listed because a change here would be OUT OF CONTRACT. ***
classification.ADDITIVE: a new symbol/field/verb. Old callers unaffected.
classification.BEHAVIORAL: existing code, changed semantics. *** The dangerous class. ***
classification.COMPATIBILITY: changes what an OLD binary or an OLD on-disk shape can do.
classification.MIGRATION: requires a one-time data or operational step.
classification.MANUAL: hand-maintained; a human must update it.

modules.detail: *** Replace `_file_lock(cfg.lock_path)` + `store.restore()` (os.replace) with `store.lock()` + `store.write_raw(snap_doc)` — IN PLACE, SAME INODE. Retain os.replace as the CORRUPT-DB fallback. ***
*** ALREADY CORRECT. `Ledger.snapshot` (:540) is the MODEL — it already takes store.lock(). DO NOT TOUCH ANY OF THESE. ***
Extract the body of `read_raw`, parametrized on path. ~8 lines. *** ZERO behaviour change. ***

*** `write_raw` ALREADY performs a full-document DELETE+INSERT and, when `self._conn` is set (own=False), REUSES THE CALLER'S LOCKED CONNECTION WITHOUT COMMITTING — exactly the semantics required. NOTHING IS INVENTED. ***

persistence.detail: *** HOW it is restored changes. WHAT is stored does not. NO schema change. NO on-disk format change. ***
*** FORMAT UNCHANGED. A post-fix snapshot restores on PRE-fix code — this is what makes the revert safe. ***

concurrency.detail: *** restore_snapshot JOINS the L1 domain. It currently participates in NO domain. ***
*** Loses its SOLE consumer and becomes GENUINELY DEAD for the first time — three cycles after being WRONGLY filed as dead (DEAD-005). DO NOT DELETE IT IN THIS PR; bundling obscures the diff. ***

side_effects.detail: *** REMOVED from the happy path. RETAINED for the corrupt-db fallback. ***
*** UNCHANGED — but the RACE that made them delete live media is CLOSED. This is the severity driver. ***

tests.class: *** BEHAVIORAL — A REWRITE. IT CURRENTLY ASSERTS THE DATA LOSS (RC-5). ***

NOT_affected: *** ANY other ledger writer. The 74 Ledger.transaction sites. The 8 entity maps. The migration chain. ***

modules.detail: *** The EXACT rule set_backend already applies: `.strip().lower()`; None if not in _VALID_BACKENDS. NOT NEW POLICY — it moves an existing rule to the door that lacks it. ***
Apply normalize_backend. An unknown name -> drop the channel's backend + record it in the EXISTING `skipped_rows`.
Add the VALUE check to the existing PAIRING check.
Call normalize_backend; delete its inline copy.
Harden the live-guard to MEMBERSHIP as defence in depth. *** With load normalizing, this becomes UNREACHABLE — WHICH IS THE POINT. ***

*** COUP-05: it is defined TWICE (config.py:72, settings.py:18) — one guards the WRITE boundary, one the READ boundary. If they DRIFT, the two boundaries disagree about what a legal backend IS. That is the gap RC-3 exploits. ***

configuration.detail: *** `golive._dual_write` is its ONLY setter (INV-18). S02 does not touch it. ***

persistence.class: *** READ_ONLY ON DISK. Normalization happens at LOAD, in memory. The file is NOT rewritten — which is what makes the revert clean. ***

modules.detail: *** Handle 404 BEFORE the generic >= 300 raise. A 404 semantically IS 'the backend does not know this id' — that is `unknown`, NOT an error. ***

unknowns: *** Whether Zernio returns 404 (vs 410, vs 200-with-error) for a DELETED post. `post/metrics.py`'s own docstring: the shapes are 'locked offline here; the operator verifies live at first publish.' IF YOU CANNOT VERIFY IT LIVE, SAY SO. ***
